features.name: Agent Harness Baseline
features.description: Install a minimal restartable harness with startup instructions, feature state, progress logging, verification, and handoff flow.
done_criteria: AGENTS.md routes startup, scope, verification, and end-of-session behavior
feature_list.json has one active feature and concrete status/evidence fields
progress.md records current state, next step, risks, files, and verification evidence
AGENTS.md documents the lightweight SwiftPM verification gate without a startup script
session-handoff.md gives the next session a restartable path
features.evidence: Harness implemented without a startup script per user request. Structural validation: 88/100; bottleneck is verification because the bundled validator expects a startup script. feature_list.json is valid JSON. Direct SwiftPM verification is documented but could not be completed in the managed sandbox after escalation was rejected.

features.name: Session Voice Activity Indicator UI
features.description: Add a four-circle macOS-soft voice activity indicator to the Session panel, remove the gray Session card, keep the live session ayah aligned with recitation progress, and show the Mushaf page number at the page bottom.
done_criteria: Session summary renders without the gray material card while preserving metadata alignment
Voice activity view is a four-circle macOS-soft indicator that steps only while recording
Session ayah label follows the reciter's current focused ayah
Mushaf page shows a centered page number at the bottom without changing scroll or page metrics
Completion evidence is captured in progress.md
features.evidence: Implemented on 2026-06-16 and refined on 2026-06-17. Red check: `swift test --filter RecitationViewModelTests/testDisplayedAyahFollowsNextTrackedAyahDuringRecitation` initially failed because `displayedAyah` did not exist. Follow-up replacement red check: `swift test --filter VoiceActivityIndicatorTests` failed because `VoiceActivityIndicatorMetrics` did not exist. Removed the custom blob layout and wired a private four-circle macOS-soft indicator to `viewModel.isRecording`; final checks: `swift test` passed 77 tests with 1 existing opt-in local audio audit skipped; `swift build` completed successfully; cleanup search found no `RecitationVoiceBlobLayout` or `RecitationWaveformView(` matches in HifzTracker/Tests.

features.name: Live ASR Timing Instrumentation
features.description: Add lightweight on-device timing logs for the live ASR path so first transcript latency, transcript cadence, pending-window handoffs, and ASR processing time can be measured before choosing the next optimization.
done_criteria: Recording start emits a live ASR timing marker
Each ASR window logs start and finish timing with processing duration
First transcript latency is reported once per recording session
Transcript interval and average interval are reported after multiple transcripts
Pending-window storage and pending handoff starts are logged separately
Timing math is covered by focused unit tests
Completion evidence is captured in progress.md
features.evidence: Implemented on 2026-06-17. Added `LiveASRTimingProbe`, wired `RecitationViewModel` to emit `live_asr_timing` OSLog events for recording start, transcription start/finish, pending-window storage, pending-window handoff start, and highlight application. Final checks: `swift test --filter LiveASRTimingProbeTests` passed 3 tests; `swift test` passed 80 tests with 1 existing opt-in local audio audit skipped; `swift build` completed successfully.

features.name: Live ASR Locator Outcome Instrumentation
features.description: Add lightweight locator outcome diagnostics to the live ASR path so transcript-to-highlight gaps can be explained by no match, short initial lock, repeated non-advancing matches, empty transcripts, missing references, or successful progress.
done_criteria: Progressive transcript locating exposes diagnostic outcomes without changing existing locate behavior
Each live ASR transcript logs one locator outcome with recognized word count and expected reference count
Successful locator outcomes log matched word count and completed surah/ayah/word
Rejected locator outcomes log actionable no-progress reasons
No transcript text or audio is persisted in logs
Outcome mapping and locator diagnostics are covered by focused unit tests
Completion evidence is captured in progress.md
features.evidence: Implemented on 2026-06-17. Added `ProgressiveTranscriptLocatorOutcome`, `LiveASRLocatorOutcomeProbe`, and `live_asr_locator event=locator_outcome` OSLog events in `RecitationViewModel`. Focused red checks failed for missing diagnostic APIs before implementation, then focused checks passed 13 tests with 0 failures. Final full verification is recorded in progress.md.

features.name: Guarded 3-Word Initial Lock
features.description: Reduce first-highlight latency by allowing the initial locator lock to accept a unique near-start 3-word match while preserving the default 4-word strong lock and existing post-lock behavior.
done_criteria: ProgressiveTranscriptLocator keeps minimumInitialMatchLength defaulted to 4
A unique 3-word initial match near the selected start is accepted
Repeated or ambiguous 3-word initial matches are rejected until a 4-word match appears
Unique 3-word initial matches at or beyond the first 32 expected words are rejected
Complete short ayah initial lock and post-lock distant short-match protection are preserved
Completion evidence is captured in progress.md
features.evidence: Implemented on 2026-06-17. Red check: `swift test --filter ProgressiveTranscriptLocatorTests` failed because `testAcceptsUniqueThreeWordInitialMatchNearStart` still returned nil. Added a private guarded first-lock fallback for exactly 3 normalized words when the phrase is unique in the selected reference scope and starts before expected offset 32. Final checks: focused locator tests passed 9 tests; outcome tests passed 3 tests; `swift test` passed 89 tests with 1 existing opt-in local audio audit skipped; `swift build` completed successfully.

features.name: Post-Lock Advancing Locator Preference
features.description: Reduce post-lock highlight stalls by preferring locator candidates that complete beyond the accepted offset before falling back to the existing strongest-match behavior.
done_criteria: Post-lock locating first searches for candidates that advance beyond the accepted offset
Older stronger matches no longer block a shorter advancing match from applying progress
Existing no-match and not-advancing diagnostics remain available when no advancing candidate exists
Initial-lock behavior, ASR cadence, scheduler, repository, and Mushaf rendering are unchanged
Completion evidence is captured in progress.md
features.evidence: Implemented on 2026-06-17. Red check: `swift test --filter ProgressiveTranscriptLocatorTests` failed because `testPostLockPrefersAdvancingMatchOverStrongerOldMatch` returned nil when a stronger old match beat a shorter advancing match. Added an optional completing-after filter to `TranscriptPositionLocator.locate` and used it only after `ProgressiveTranscriptLocator` has an accepted offset. Final checks: focused locator tests passed 10 tests; outcome tests passed 3 tests; transcript locator tests passed 3 tests; `swift test` passed 90 tests with 1 existing opt-in local audio audit skipped; `swift build` completed successfully. Live log review from the 2026-06-17 16:38 run showed healthy ASR timing (`first_transcript_latency_ms=1097.487792`, window 156 `average_transcript_interval_ms=502.281718`, `processing_ms=444.147084`), no ASR/start failures, and no pending-window backlog. The guarded 3-word initial lock applied at window 16, about 8.94s after recording start, because earlier windows were no-match or only 2-word short initial matches. Locator outcomes across 157 windows: 54 `progress_applied`, 87 `not_advancing`, 9 `no_match`, 3 `initial_match_too_short`, 3 `empty_transcript`, and 1 final `not_recording`.

features.name: Guarded Provisional Initial Highlight
features.description: Show a visual-only provisional first highlight after the same unique 2-word near-start candidate appears in consecutive live ASR windows, without committing recitation progress until the existing locator confirms.
done_criteria: WordProgressState supports a distinct provisional visual state in SwiftUI and rendered Mushaf pages
Core tracker confirms only exact 2-word candidates that are unique across the selected reference scope and start before the first 16 expected words
Replacement or missing evidence clears stale provisional state before a new candidate can be confirmed
3+ word matches and candidates at or beyond the guarded start limit stay owned by the normal locator path
RecitationViewModel applies provisional highlights as visual-only state without advancing snapshot.completedWordCount or calling applyLocatedProgress
Real located progress clears provisional visuals and applies authoritative completed/current states
Lifecycle, ASR error, empty transcript/reference, and cross-page cleanup paths do not orphan provisional states
Completion evidence is captured in progress.md
features.evidence: Implemented on 2026-06-17 on branch `provisional-initial-highlight`. Added `.provisional` word state rendering, `ProvisionalInitialHighlightTracker`, live ASR view-model wiring, provisional locator logs, and focused safety tests. Final checks: provisional tracker tests passed 8 tests; progressive locator tests passed 10 tests; RecitationViewModelTests passed 12 tests; full `swift test` passed 103 tests with 1 expected local-audio audit skipped and 0 failures; `swift build` completed successfully. On-device log verification is still pending.

features.name: Surah Boundary Live Highlight Continuation
features.description: Allow live ASR highlighting to continue from the selected surah into the immediate next surah, so finishing surah 100 and reciting surah 101 keeps page flips and word highlights moving.
done_criteria: Live ASR reference scope includes the selected surah from startAyah and the immediate next surah
Post-lock locating can advance from the final ayah of the selected surah into the next surah
The Mushaf page flips to the next surah page when the next tracked word is there
Next-surah word states render completed and current highlights after a boundary transcript

Red check: `swift test --filter RecitationViewModelTests/testLiveASRHighlightsNextSurahAfterCompletingSelectedSurah` first failed because the live transcript method was private, then failed behaviorally because the surah 101 transcript returned false, page stayed at 100, and surah 101 word states stayed pending. Extended `RecitationViewModel` live reference scope to the immediate next surah and updated selected-ayah sync for later-surah completions. Final checks: focused boundary regression passed 1 test; RecitationViewModelTests passed 13 tests; `swift test` passed 104 tests with 1 expected local-audio audit skipped and 0 failures; `swift build` completed successfully.

features.name: Guarded Short Ayah Initial Lock
features.description: Prevent initial live ASR locking from jumping to a later short ayah and preserve visible ayah marker glyphs while Hide Ayah suppresses unrecited words.
done_criteria: A later complete short ayah cannot initial-lock before the locator has accepted the selected start neighborhood
Nearby complete short ayahs near the selected start remain accepted
Existing guarded 3-word initial lock and post-lock advancing behavior remain covered
Hide-mode Mushaf rendering clips a full-line draw to visible word slots so hidden text is suppressed without per-word glyph redraws
Hide-mode Mushaf renderer coverage confirms a revealed ayah retains visible marker ink when following words are hidden
Completion evidence is captured in progress.md
features.evidence: Diagnosed on 2026-06-17 after a manual Hide Ayah test showed missing ayah numbers and a jump to later ayahs. Red check: `swift test --filter ProgressiveTranscriptLocatorTests/testRejectsLaterCompleteShortAyahBeforeInitialLock` failed because a confused Al-Ghashiyah transcript (`وجوه يومئذ ناعمة`) initial-locked ayah 88:8 at expected range 28..<31. Fixes: the complete-short-ayah initial-lock exception is now limited to the near-start neighborhood, and hidden QPC text now draws the full line once clipped to visible word slots so ayah marker glyph behavior is preserved without leaking hidden target text. Focused checks and full SwiftPM verification passed, then the fix was committed and pushed. Follow-up manual testing showed this did not fix every marker disappearance because QPC ayah-number rows can remain pending in view-model state; see `hide-ayah-marker-state-order-001`.

features.name: Hide Ayah Marker State and Ordered Initial Lock
features.description: Fix residual Hide Ayah failures where QPC ayah-number marker rows stayed hidden after an ayah completed and initial live ASR could lock to a far repeated phrase instead of following the selected ayah order.
done_criteria: QPC ayah-number marker rows reveal when the final real word of their ayah is completed, provisional, uncertain, or correction-needed
Pending/current QPC marker rows remain hidden with their ayah in Hide Ayah mode
Initial live ASR locks cannot jump to far repeated phrases before progress from the selected start neighborhood is established
Starting from a later selected ayah still works because the guarded offset is relative to the selected reference scope
Surah 110 trailing transcripts ending with `توابا` complete the final real word in deterministic locator coverage
Locator outcome diagnostics report `initial_match_too_far` for rejected far initial locks
Completion evidence is captured in progress.md
features.evidence: Implemented on 2026-06-17 on main after screenshots showed missing ayah numbers, a Surah 98 jump to ayah 6, and a Surah 110 final-word concern. Database check showed QPC rows include extra marker rows such as `88:1:5` and `110:3:8`, while normalized reference text has 4 and 7 real words respectively. Red checks: `testRejectsFarRepeatedStrongMatchBeforeInitialLock` initially accepted Surah 98 ayah 6 at expected range 48..<56; `testHideRecitationTextRevealsCompletedAyahMarkerRows` initially kept the marker hidden. The Surah 110 locator checks passed for both initial-lock and post-lock trailing transcripts ending at `110:3:7` (`توابا`), so the deterministic core matcher does not drop that word. Fixes: QPC marker rows inherit the final real word's visible feedback state, and first live ASR lock rejects matches starting at expected offset 32 or later before any accepted progress. Focused regression check passed 4 tests; full `swift test` passed 128 tests with 1 expected local-audio skip and 0 failures; `swift build` completed successfully; `./script/build_and_run.sh --verify` exited 0 after rebuilding, signing, and relaunching `dist/HifzTracker.app`.

features.name: Post-Lock Single Substitution Bridge
features.description: Allow live ASR progress to finish a short ayah after the locator is already locked when one ASR word is substituted in the middle but the accepted prefix and trailing suffix match.
done_criteria: Live locator logs for the screenshot are diagnosed and recorded
A focused failing regression reproduces Surah 96:8 stalling after `إن إلى` when ASR returns `إن إلى ربه الرجعى`
Post-lock locating can bridge exactly one substituted word immediately after the accepted offset when at least two accepted prefix words repeat and at least one suffix word matches
The bridge is constrained to the same ayah and does not run before initial lock
The bridge does not run when the repeated accepted prefix is absent
Existing first-lock jump protections and post-lock advancing tests remain green
Completion evidence is captured in progress.md
features.evidence: Implemented on 2026-06-17 on main after Surah 96 / Al-Alaq page 597 showed Debug Transcript `إن إلى ربه الرجعى` while Mushaf Hide Ayah revealed only `إن إلى`. Logs showed the exact stall: window 165 `progress_applied` to `completed_surah=96 completed_ayah=8 completed_word=2`, followed by windows 166+ returning `not_advancing` with `completed_offset=62 accepted_offset=62`. Red check: `testPostLockCompletesShortAyahAcrossSingleASRSubstitutionWithMatchedSuffix` initially returned nil for `إن إلى ربه الرجعى`; green check completes `96:8:4`. Added a safety test proving the bridge does not fire without the repeated accepted prefix. Focused locator suite passed 17 tests; full `swift test` passed 130 tests with 1 expected local-audio skip and 0 failures; `swift build` completed successfully; `./script/build_and_run.sh --verify` exited 0 after rebuilding, signing, and relaunching `dist/HifzTracker.app`.

Browser verification served `docs/` locally and confirmed desktop 1280x720 and mobile 390x844 render with images loaded, stable latest-release download URL, visible next-section hint, and no horizontal overflow. `./script/release_checks.sh release` passed tests/builds/staged bundle signing checks but exited 1 at release-only signing assessment; `security find-identity -p codesigning -v` reported 0 valid identities and `spctl -a -vv dist/HifzTracker.app` returned `internal error in Code Signing subsystem`. `gh auth status` reported the moabdelmoez token is invalid, so GitHub Release upload and Pages settings activation remain externally blocked.

features.name: Live ASR Performance Feedback Loop
features.description: Make first-highlight performance measurable with an app-faithful local replay and privacy-safe startup, voice-onset, transcript, and highlight timing events before changing ASR behavior.
done_criteria: The opt-in local audio audit uses the production 1-to-8-second rolling sample window and selected start ayah
The audit reports time to first transcript, provisional highlight, and authoritative highlight
Live timing distinguishes start request, ASR service readiness, reference readiness, microphone readiness, voice onset, first transcript, and first highlight
Timing logs contain no transcript text or audio
Model, inference cadence, locator acceptance, and user-facing recitation behavior remain unchanged
Completion evidence is captured in progress.md
features.evidence: Completed on 2026-07-14. Red/green coverage added an app-faithful local replay and startup-to-highlight timing boundaries. The opt-in audit passed all 6 fixtures across 341 production-shaped rolling windows in 33.696 s: first transcript was about 1.04 s, first authoritative highlight ranged from 4.148 s to 7.779 s, processing p50 was 102.21 ms, and processing p95 was 113.98 ms. Focused timing/window tests passed; final `swift test` passed 124 tests with 1 expected opt-in audit skip and 0 failures; `swift build` and `./script/build_and_run.sh --verify` passed. Logs expose only elapsed timings and counts, never transcript text or audio. No model, inference cadence, locator acceptance, or user-facing behavior changed. A targeted automated live-log capture could not send Command-R because macOS denied `osascript` Accessibility control with error 1002; the staged app launch itself passed, and manual recitation remains the next live validation step.

features.name: Locator Ayah Jump Diagnosis
features.description: Diagnose the reported live locator jump from ayah 3 to ayah 10 using unified logs and a deterministic replay before changing locator behavior.
done_criteria: The exact live transition from ayah 3 to ayah 10 is captured from logs
A deterministic locator replay reproduces the reported transition or the missing replay input is documented
Three to five falsifiable hypotheses are ranked and tested against evidence
The root cause and smallest safe next action are recorded without speculative locator changes
Completion evidence is captured in progress.md
verification: targeted unified-log review for locator outcomes and applied progress
features.evidence: Completed on 2026-07-14. Unified logs reproduce the exact transition: window 18 completed 7:3:14, then monotonic window 19 reused a 14-word transcript and accepted a 2-word match at 7:10:10. A temporary deterministic test using the canonical 7:3-10 reference first failed with `expectedRange 76..<78`, `recognizedRange 11..<13`, and completed location 7:10:10. The cause probe then passed and proved the normal contiguous matcher—not the single-gap bridge or UI mapping—selects the repeated phrase `قليلا ما`; increasing the matcher minimum from 2 to 3 removes this repro. Root cause: after 7:3 is accepted, the rolling transcript still contains that completed ayah; `completingAfter` rejects its old location, while the default 96-word look-ahead accepts the repeated 2-word phrase in 7:10 as advancement. A global 3-word minimum was not applied because valid nearby post-lock progression intentionally supports 2-word matches. Baseline `swift test` passed 124 tests with 1 expected skip and 0 failures; `swift build` passed. After temporary-probe cleanup, all 17 focused locator tests, `jq empty feature_list.json`, the debug-marker sweep, and `git diff --check` passed. No runtime behavior or user audio was changed.

features.name: Sequential Ayah Progression
features.description: Constrain live locator progress to the current ayah and its immediate Quran-order successor so repeated phrases cannot skip intervening ayahs.
done_criteria: A canonical regression prevents the observed 7:3 to 7:10 jump
Each locator call can complete only the current ayah or its immediate next ayah
Repeated rolling transcripts advance through ayahs in Quran order without skipping
The rule works across a surah boundary
Nearby two-word progression within the allowed ayah scope remains supported
Completion evidence is captured in progress.md
features.evidence: Completed on 2026-07-14. Red coverage reproduced the canonical 7:3:14 to 7:10:10 jump and showed multi-ayah skips within surah 110 and across 100:11 to 101:2. `ProgressiveTranscriptLocator` now limits every matching path to the current ayah and its immediate Quran-order successor using `SurahCatalog`, while an unrestricted initial probe still rejects a stronger distant match instead of accepting a weaker local similarity. Repeated windows therefore advance 1 to 2 to 3 one call at a time, including across a surah boundary; nearby 2-word matches and substitution recovery remain supported. The focused locator suite passed 19 tests, locator outcomes passed 3 tests, and the strengthened opt-in local-audio audit passed all 6 fixtures in 32.589 s with every located ayah confined to its target. Final `swift test` passed 126 tests with 1 expected opt-in skip and 0 failures in 44.005 s; `swift build`, `jq empty feature_list.json`, the debug-marker sweep, and `git diff --check` passed. No model, inference cadence, audio persistence, or release assets changed.

features.name: Dashboard Reset and Mushaf Navigation
features.description: Add a confirmed dashboard progress reset, a native Start ayah picker, a non-overlapping Mushaf page footer, and hover-revealed RTL page navigation.
done_criteria: Dashboard can delete all saved progress after a native destructive confirmation without affecting an active in-memory recitation
Start ayah uses a numeric native menu picker for the selected surah
Mushaf page numbers retain a clear reserved footer band below the final ayah
Hover-revealed reading-pane controls and plain arrow keys browse pages in RTL direction without changing recitation setup
Manual browsing during recording yields to automatic page following on the next located progress
Completion evidence is captured in progress.md
features.evidence: Implemented on 2026-07-14. Added a native confirmed reset that deletes all saved SwiftData session records with rollback/error handling, replaced the Start ayah Stepper with a numeric menu Picker, reserved an 80-point canonical footer band on page 574 and other dense pages, and added hover-revealed RTL page controls with plain arrow-key shortcuts. Manual browsing preserves recitation setup, respects pages 1...604, remains available while recording, and yields to live auto-follow on the next located word. Red checks initially failed for the missing reset/page commands; focused green checks passed 5 tests, relevant UI/rendering suites passed 43 tests, and final `swift test` passed 131 tests with 1 expected opt-in skip and 0 failures. `swift build` and `./script/build_and_run.sh --verify` passed. No release assets, schema, dependencies, or audio behavior changed; release checks were therefore not required. Per user instruction, no screenshot verification was performed.

features.name: Live ASR Runtime and Model Assessment
features.description: Investigate the 2026-07-14 live recitation log end to end, identify real-time bottlenecks, confirm the active model precision, and assess INT8 and streaming alternatives without changing runtime behavior.
done_criteria: The live session startup, audio, inference, locator, and highlight path is traced from code and unified logs
Session timing, scheduler pressure, locator outcomes, and completed Quran locations are quantified
The active model format and available local precision variants are verified
INT8, updated-weight, and true-streaming options are ranked against observed bottlenecks and accuracy risk
No source, model, asset, persisted audio, or runtime behavior is changed
Completion evidence is captured in progress.md
verification: targeted 2026-07-14 unified-log review for ASR and Microphone categories
features.evidence: Completed on 2026-07-14 as a read-only investigation. The 14:37:54-14:39:21 live session processed 149 rolling windows with 130.312 ms mean ASR time (42.304-199.312 ms), 568.632 ms mean transcript interval, 136 full 8-second windows, no pending-window backlog, and no ASR/microphone failures. First transcript arrived 1.152 s after recording; first authoritative highlight arrived 3.504 s after voice onset after six no-match windows. Locator outcomes were 56 progress_applied, 80 not_advancing, 11 no_match, 1 empty_transcript, and 1 post-stop not_recording; all applied progress was sequential in Surah 7 from 7:3 through 7:11, ending at 7:11:16. The logged 4,570-word reference scope exactly matches selected Surah 7 from ayah 3 plus Surah 8, not intended Surah 6 from ayah 3 plus Surah 7 (6,369 words), while the picker itself uses one-based Surah numbers. AppASRFactory hardcodes bundled model_fp32.onnx; ONNX Runtime 1.26.0 uses CPU with 2 intra-op threads and full graph optimization. A dynamic INT8 ONNX is present locally (166 MB versus 437 MB FP32) but is not bundled or benchmarked. Existing FP32 fixture evidence attributes about 54.4 ms/window to feature extraction, 28.9 ms to inference, and 7.6 ms to decode, so quantization cannot by itself remove the multi-second initial locator delay. Baseline rerun with temporary compiler caches passed 131 tests with 1 expected opt-in skip and 0 failures; swift build passed. No Swift, model, runtime, asset, or user audio was changed.

done_criteria: Recitation toolbar and Session metadata no longer duplicate title or live status

features.evidence: Completed on 2026-07-16. Baseline `swift test` passed 131 tests with 1 expected opt-in local-audio skip and 0 failures; baseline `swift build` passed. A focused red check proved the four-circle indicator required 280 points inside the 208-point minimum sidebar content width. The final 204-point layout passed all 4 VoiceActivityIndicator tests. Recitation now keeps status in the toolbar, consolidates Session metadata with tabular ayah/page numbers, removes the duplicate navigation title, and respects Reduce Motion for the voice indicator, page transitions, and focus scrolling. Dashboard now uses a native no-progress empty state; Settings uses consistent grouped forms; the white Mushaf page has a neutral 0.5-point inset outline. Focused Mushaf canvas tests passed 5 tests and Dashboard reset passed 1 test. Final `swift test` passed 132 tests with 1 expected skip and 0 failures; `swift build` and `./script/build_and_run.sh --verify` passed. `jq empty feature_list.json`, scoped symbol sweeps, and `git diff --check` passed. Automated on-screen inspection was not claimed because macOS rejected the app-only capture with `could not create image from rect`; manual light/dark review remains available. Release checks were skipped because no release assets, signing, packaging, dependencies, schema, model, or audio behavior changed.

features.name: Strict Fresh Ayah Ordering
features.description: Require live locator progress to finish the current ayah before entering its Quran-order successor and prevent repeated words from an overlapping ASR window being reused as next-ayah evidence.
done_criteria: Initial and provisional locating stay inside the selected start ayah
Each locator update can advance within only one ayah, including across surah boundaries
The next ayah requires word evidence spoken after the current ayah final word
Fresh post-boundary words captured in the same audio window remain usable on a later update
Missing or invalid word timing holds progress and emits a privacy-safe diagnostic
Completion evidence is captured in progress.md
features.evidence: Completed on 2026-07-16. Baseline `swift test` passed 132 tests with 1 expected skip and 0 failures; baseline `swift build` passed. Unified logs and a deterministic replay reproduced unfinished Surah 72 transitions and isolated the current-or-successor search range as the cause. The locator now searches only the selected/current ayah, advances at most one ayah per update, and requires frame-timed word evidence after the accepted final-word sample boundary before entering the successor (including across surahs). CTC frame ranges are preserved through tokenizer word assembly and mapped through absolute rolling-window sample ranges; missing or inconsistent timing holds progress and logs `invalid_word_timing` without storing audio/transcripts or presenting an error UI. Regressions prove stale overlap is rejected and fresh words later in the same rolling window remain usable on a later update. Focused locator/provisional suites passed 30 tests; the opt-in local audio/model audit passed 1 test in 32.703 s. Final `swift test` passed 140 tests with 1 expected opt-in skip and 0 failures in 45.244 s; `swift build`, `jq empty feature_list.json`, and `git diff --check` passed. Release checks were skipped because no release assets, signing, packaging, dependencies, or distribution inputs changed. Follow-up diagnosis on 2026-07-17 found the reported Surah 10 jump in a mounted July 15 DMG binary that predates this fix: logs showed `10:5:16 -> 10:6:8`, while ayah 5 has 23 reference words. The running executable path was `/Volumes/Hifz Tracker/HifzTracker.app/Contents/MacOS/HifzTracker`; that binary lacks the new `fresh_evidence_required` and `invalid_word_timing` strings, while the current debug build contains them. The current focused unfinished-ayah regression, all 140 tests, and `swift build` passed. No source change was required; packaged artifacts must be rebuilt before retesting or distribution.

features.name: Lower-Latency Live ASR Cadence
features.description: Reduce perceived live locator latency by emitting ASR windows every quarter second and limiting rolling audio context to five seconds, without weakening strict ayah-order safeguards.
done_criteria: Default live ASR windows emit after one second and every quarter second thereafter
Default rolling audio context is capped at five seconds
Custom window configuration and strict fresh-ayah locator behavior remain unchanged
Focused and full SwiftPM verification passes
Completion evidence is captured in progress.md
features.evidence: Implemented on 2026-07-18 after live Surah 6 diagnosis measured 568.7 ms transcript cadence with 128.8 ms average inference, no pending-window backlog, and 131 rejected locator outcomes across 174 windows. The prepared 6,000-word locator benchmark passed 360 locates in 25 ms, isolating rolling ASR freshness and acceptance outcomes rather than locator compute as the latency source. Baseline `swift test` passed 140 tests with 1 expected skip and 0 failures; baseline `swift build` passed. Red checks proved the old defaults: the quarter-second test received no second window at 20,000 samples, and the five-second-cap test retained 96,000 rather than 80,000 samples. Changed only `LiveASRSampleWindow` defaults from 8 to 5 seconds and from 0.5 to 0.25 seconds; custom configuration and strict locator logic are unchanged. Final focused checks passed 5 sample-window tests, the production audit-window check, and 22 strict locator tests. Final `swift test` passed 140 tests with 1 expected opt-in local-audio audit skip and 0 failures; `swift build` passed. The opt-in model/audio audit was skipped because it rewrites the tracked generated audit artifact; live Surah 6 log comparison remains the next validation. Release checks were skipped because no assets, signing, packaging, dependencies, or distribution artifacts changed.

features.name: Same-Window Fresh Ayah Transition
features.description: Use fresh post-boundary evidence already present in one timed live transcript to enter the immediate successor ayah without waiting for another ASR update.
done_criteria: One timed live transcript can complete the current ayah and apply fresh progress in its immediate successor
Only evidence after the accepted final-word sample boundary can support successor progress
One transcript cannot cross more than one ayah boundary
One privacy-safe locator outcome reports the furthest location applied for each transcript
Focused and full SwiftPM verification passes
Completion evidence is captured in progress.md
features.evidence: Implemented on 2026-07-18 for GitHub issue #1. Baseline `swift test` passed 140 tests with 1 expected opt-in skip and 0 failures; baseline `swift build` passed. The focused red check failed because one timed transcript spanning surah 100:11 into 101:1 left the snapshot at ayah 11 and successor words pending. The live transcript path now evaluates the same timed evidence once more only after an ayah-final result, keeps the original result unless the second evaluation locates the immediate successor, and logs/applies only the furthest valid outcome. Cross-surah and same-surah integration regressions prove immediate fresh progress and the one-boundary ceiling. Focused view-model and strict locator suites passed 47 tests; final `swift test` passed 142 tests with 1 expected opt-in skip and 0 failures; `swift build`, `jq empty feature_list.json`, and `git diff --check` passed. A post-change Surah 10 manual run progressed sequentially from 10:1 through 10:10 with no skips; median ayah-boundary delay was 2.104 seconds, average was 2.529 seconds, and the 6.600-second worst case coincided with repeated `no_match` and `fresh_evidence_required` outcomes. No transcript in that run both completed an ayah and supplied an eligible successor match, so it verified safety but did not exercise the same-window fast path. The opt-in model/audio audit and release checks were skipped because no model, audio fixture, asset, signing, packaging, dependency, or distribution inputs changed.

features.name: Live Recitation Performance Pass
features.description: Measure the current five-second/quarter-second live pipeline and apply only threshold-justified launch and rendering improvements while leaving unproven ASR and audio changes out of scope.
done_criteria: Instruments can identify microphone handling, transcript application, and Mushaf drawing intervals
Existing privacy-safe startup, transcription, cadence, backlog, and locator logs remain available
No transcript text, audio, or user data is added to telemetry
Current runtime evidence is compared with explicit smoothness thresholds before any optimization is selected
A measured cold ONNX setup above 200 milliseconds is moved into one reusable launch task
Mushaf canvas updates invalidate drawing only when page presentation or size changes
Focused and full SwiftPM verification passes
A live microphone trace verifies the new sample and transcript intervals before completion
features.evidence: Completed on 2026-07-19. Baseline `swift test` passed 142 tests with 1 expected skip and `swift build` passed. Added privacy-safe Instruments intervals for microphone handling, transcript application, and Mushaf drawing. Three fresh ONNX test processes took 345, 335, and 333 ms, exceeding the 200 ms warm-up gate; the app now starts one reusable utility-priority ASR task and exercises it with one second of zeros. Replaced closure-identity invalidation with an equatable word-presentation snapshot; all 6 canvas tests pass. A 128-second live Surah 8 trace from the current instrumented build recorded 426 windows: processing p50 92.961 ms, p95 96.389 ms, max 117.552 ms; transcript cadence p50 299.101 ms and p95 311.659 ms; no pending-window backlog and no failures. Main-thread intervals were small: microphone/apply p95 at most 1 ms and Mushaf draw p95 3 ms; one progress application took 18 ms. Launch warm-up completed in 385.684 ms and made service acquisition 9.775 ms; reference preparation reached 262.593 ms and microphone readiness 1,803.074 ms. The first authoritative highlight arrived 7,096.941 ms after voice onset after early `no_match` outcomes. Reused the existing opt-in local audio audit, adding `HIFZ_LOCAL_AUDIO_AUDIT_FILE` to select one fixture. A deterministic 37.928-second Alafasy Basmallah plus Surah 8:1 fixture reproduced the boundary: first transcript 1,044.130 ms, first authoritative progress at 11,066.791 ms on normalized `يسالونك عن الانفال`, then correct completion through 8:1:18. The ayah-only control produced a provisional highlight at 2,338.223 ms and authoritative progress at 4,147.655 ms, isolating roughly 6.92 seconds of leading Basmallah/transition rather than compute or locator runtime. Final focused replay passed; final `swift test` passed 143 tests with 1 expected skip and 0 failures; `swift build`, JSON validation, and diff checks passed. The ignored local fixture has SHA-256 `c76b8542f346ee35eb1bdab11a56498fbd86bf70934c4d922e15c2ca86b7b0fb`. No user audio was persisted and no production locator, audio, model, asset, signing, packaging, or release behavior changed.

features.name: Surah 60:1-5 Offline Replay Report
features.description: Replay the supplied local Surah 60 ayahs 1-5 recording through production ASR windows and identify whether latency is caused by recognition, locating, cadence, or compute.
done_criteria: The existing audit supports a bounded ayah range without changing single-ayah behavior
The supplied M4A is replayed through production rolling windows without retaining a duplicate audio file
First transcript, provisional highlight, authoritative highlight, ayah progression, accuracy, and processing timing are measured
A privacy-safe Markdown report states the main bottleneck and next step
Focused and full SwiftPM verification passes
features.evidence: Completed on 2026-07-19. Extended the opt-in local audio audit with `HIFZ_LOCAL_AUDIO_AUDIT_END_AYAH` while preserving its single-ayah default, and corrected the range summary to include matched references from every requested ayah. Replayed the supplied 97.408-second Surah 60:1-5 M4A as a temporary mono 16 kHz PCM fixture across 377 production-style rolling windows. The focused replay passed in 24.375 seconds; summed ASR work was 23.469 seconds (0.241x realtime), first transcript was 1,046.378 ms, and first authoritative highlight was 5,952.165 ms. The locator ended at 60:5:13 and merged expected-word LCS recall was 139/140. Per-window total p50/p95/max was 62.947/65.131/72.509 ms against a 255.938 ms cadence, so compute, rendering, and cadence are not the primary bottleneck. At 11.835 seconds the locator reached 60:1:12, then at 12.348 seconds a repeated two-word phrase falsely advanced it to 60:1:36 and caused a 15.356-second stall before 60:1:38. The diagnosis is a locator continuity/candidate-selection defect, not primarily model/decoder or normalization. The privacy-safe report is `docs/surah-60-1-5-offline-replay.md`; it recommends a focused repeated-phrase regression before the smallest locator fix. The temporary WAV, symlink, and transcript-bearing generated report were removed; the original source remained unchanged with SHA-256 `aeb8145107c6a9b48fc9b8f8c07fcc466462b970c3604744695083f21028351a`. Final `swift test` passed 143 tests with 1 expected skip and 0 failures; `swift build` passed. Release checks were skipped because no bundled asset, signing, packaging, or distribution input changed.

features.name: Same-Ayah Repeated-Phrase Continuity
features.description: Prevent stale rolling transcripts from advancing to a later occurrence of the same phrase within one ayah while preserving adjacent repeated phrases and unique post-lock catch-up.
done_criteria: A canonical Surah 60:1 regression prevents the observed word 12 to word 36 jump
Adjacent repeated phrases and unique post-lock advancing matches remain supported
Existing sequential-ayah and strict fresh-evidence behavior remains unchanged
The Surah 60:1-5 replay advances without the repeated-phrase stall and still reaches 60:5:13

features.evidence: Implemented on 2026-07-20. The focused red regression reproduced the exact defect by locating the stale first-occurrence transcript at 60:1:36 with expected range 34..<36 after progress had reached word 12. Added one private post-lock guard that rejects only discontinuous matches whose complete normalized phrase occurs more than once in the current search range; adjacent repeated phrases, unique catch-up matches, ayah ordering, and fresh-evidence behavior are unchanged. The regression passed after the fix; all 23 ProgressiveTranscriptLocatorTests and 3 outcome tests passed. The deterministic 377-window Surah 60:1-5 replay passed in 24.546 seconds with zero false 60:1:36 outcomes, resumed from word 12 to word 15 in 1.536 seconds, retained 139/140 expected-word LCS coverage, and finished at 60:5:13. Final `swift test` passed 144 tests with 1 expected opt-in skip and 0 failures; `swift build`, JSON validation, and diff checks passed. The source M4A hash remained `aeb8145107c6a9b48fc9b8f8c07fcc466462b970c3604744695083f21028351a`; the temporary WAV was removed and the generated audit JSON was restored byte-for-byte. Release checks were skipped because no assets, signing, packaging, dependencies, model, audio persistence, or distribution inputs changed.

Completion evidence is captured in progress.md
features.evidence: Completed on 2026-07-23. Diagnosis isolated the regression to commit `b04c616`, which changed page-following from the next reference to the final confirmed word and rewrote the boundary test to expect the old page. The restored regression failed with page 1 instead of page 2. `applyLocatedProgress` now keeps focus and visual state on the confirmed word while page navigation follows the next reference when available. The focused regression passed, all 25 RecitationViewModelTests passed, full `swift test` passed 144 tests with 1 expected opt-in skip and 0 failures, and `swift build` passed. JSON and diff validation passed. Release checks were skipped because no assets, signing, packaging, dependencies, or distribution inputs changed; the existing ignored DMG was not refreshed.

features.name: Page-Boundary Auto-Flip DMG Refresh
features.description: Publish the automatic page-turn fix and replace the local free-path DMG with a verified ad-hoc build from the pushed source commit.
done_criteria: The page-boundary fix is committed and pushed to origin/main
The local release gate passes and stages the current app
The existing ignored DMG is replaced with the updated ad-hoc app
The DMG checksum, mounted signature, executable hash, and launch verify
Artifact evidence and free-path limitations are recorded
verification: mount, verify app signature and executable checksum, launch, detach
features.evidence: Completed on 2026-07-23 from pushed source commit `c01bd41`. `./script/release_checks.sh` passed 144 tests with 1 expected opt-in skip and 0 failures, rebuilt and launched the staged app, validated assets and rpaths, and verified its ad-hoc signature. The existing ignored DMG was replaced only after the temporary image passed `hdiutil verify`; the final image is 521,226,492 bytes with SHA-256 `df999c6f7cb777cbd8b13755171aae7dc52ea1434f76653a6f6e372b32f278de` and verified CRC32 `$46636848`. The mounted app passed deep strict signature verification, its executable matched the staged SHA-256 `ac79346fa903c585140443b25ef0d39cf62a15c7c039828d40a0700e90222ae6`, and it launched from the read-only image as PID 17667. The verification process was stopped, the image detached, and temporary directories removed. No Developer ID identity is installed, so the app remains ad-hoc signed and the DMG is unsigned and not notarized.
